steps.title: Recognize complementary angles
steps.content:
The problem states $R + S = 90°$, so angles $R$ and $S$ are complementary. By the cofunction identity, when two angles sum to $90°$:
$$\sin(R) = \cos(S)$$

steps.title: Apply the given sine value
steps.content:
We're given $\sin(R) = \frac{4\sqrt{2}}{9}$. Using the cofunction identity:
$$\cos(S) = \frac{4\sqrt{2}}{9}$$
That matches choice A — A is correct.
